name: Radio Equipment Stack
prompt:
Vietnam War era military radio equipment stack. AN/PRC-77 radio and accessories as used by RTOs (Radio Telephone Operators).

Physical structure:
- AN/PRC-77 radio: main unit, rectangular OD green box
  - ~0.28m x 0.08m x 0.24m
  - Front panel: dial/knob (small cylinder), frequency window, handset connector
  - Top: carrying handle
- Handset: H-250 handset beside/on top of radio
  - Old-style telephone-like handset, ~0.2m long
  - Coiled cord (simplified as thin cylinder)
- Battery: rectangular battery pack attached to bottom/back
  - Slightly smaller than radio
- Antenna: long whip antenna extending upward
  - Thin cylinder, ~2m tall, slight flex
  - Base mount on radio top
- Bag/pack: canvas carrying bag partially visible
  - OD green, rectangular soft shape

Colors: OD green radio (0x556B2F), darker controls (0x333333), handset black (0x222222), antenna silver (0x888888), canvas bag (0x6B6B4F)
Orientation: Radio sitting on ground, antenna pointing up, front toward +Z
Scale: Real-world meters (radio unit ~0.28m wide, antenna ~2m tall)
